content.problemStatement:
Chef was given an array AAA of length NNN. To calculate the score of an array, he defined a two-step process:


Multiply all the array elements to get the product, say PPP, i.e., A1√óA2√ó...√óAn=PA_1 \times A_2 \times ... \times A_n = PA1‚Äã√óA2‚Äã√ó...√óAn‚Äã=P.
Choose kkk integers [x1,x2,...,xk][x_1, x_2, ..., x_k][x1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,...,xk‚Äã] such that ‚àëi=1kxi2=P\sum_{i=1}^{k} x_i^2 = P‚àëi=1k‚Äãxi2‚Äã=P. Note that it is **not** necessary to choose distinct integers.
Then, the score of the array is defined as the minimum integer kkk satisfying the above conditions.

Chef quickly calculated the sum of scores of all the subarrays of AAA, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7. Can you do the same?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN ‚Äî the number of elements in the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,...,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of scores across all the subarrays of AAA, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: The sum of scores will be 666 because:

There are three [1][1][1] subarrays: P=1P = 1P=1, the number 111 will satisfy 12=1.1^2 = 1.12=1. Hence the score is 111.
There are two [1,1][1, 1][1,1] subarrays: P=1P = 1P=1, the number 111 will satisfy 12=1.1^2 = 1.12=1. Hence the score is 111.
For the subarray [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1], the score will also be 111.

Thus, the total score will be 6,6,6, (3√ó1+2√ó1+1(3 \times 1 + 2 \times 1 + 1(3√ó1+2√ó1+1).
Test case 2: The sum of scores will be 141414. Consider all the subarrays:

[1][1][1] - score is 1,(12=1)1, (1^2 = 1)1,(12=1).
[2][2][2] - score is 2,(12+12=2)2, (1^2 + 1^2 = 2)2,(12+12=2).
[3][3][3] - score is 3,(12+12+12=3)3, (1^2 + 1^2 + 1^2 = 3)3,(12+12+12=3).
[1,2][1, 2][1,2] - score is 2,(12+12=2)2, (1^2 + 1^2 = 2)2,(12+12=2).
[2,3][2, 3][2,3] - score is 3,(12+12+22=6)3, (1^2 + 1^2 + 2^2 = 6)3,(12+12+22=6).
[1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] - score is 3,(12+12+22=6)3, (1^2 + 1^2 + 2^2 = 6)3,(12+12+22=6).
